citation: Leung, J. R. 2026. Detecting paths of change in the heritage context: Directional motion event expression in Cantonese spoken in Toronto and Hong Kong. Asia-Pacific Language Variation 11(1-2).
publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; author-uploaded accepted manuscript
claim_scope: Directional morphemes as main verbs or complements; locative objects; source and goal; immediately following motion-cum-purpose phrases; purposive 去/嚟 boundary
important_limit: The study's coding decisions and heritage-comparison sample do not define Canto Span nodes or establish unrestricted productivity

citation: Szeto, P. Y. 2015. The Emergence of Perfective Aspect in Cantonese-English Bilingual Children: Bilingual Development and Language Contact. MPhil thesis, The Chinese University of Hong Kong.
claim_scope: Attested adult/model Cantonese motion predicates with perfective 咗 before an overt goal: 去咗圖書館, 返咗屋企, 到咗學校
important_limit: The thesis studies perfective aspect and bilingual development; these examples attest forms but do not establish a parser ontology or the full motion-verb inventory

citation: Chor, W. O. W. 2018. Directional Particles in Cantonese: Form, Function, and Grammaticalization. Amsterdam/Philadelphia: John Benjamins.
claim_scope: Directional complement ordering; 到 as arrival/goal verb or particle; spatial versus nonspatial goals; lexicalized 返工, 返學, and 返屋企 uses
important_limit: The monograph's diachronic and functional analyses do not prescribe Canto Span's runtime labels or make every directional sequence one construction

citation: Kwan, S. W. M. 2010. The Placement of Locative Prepositional Phrases in Cantonese: Processing and Iconicity. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics 8(2): 163-198.
claim_scope: Dominant preverbal 喺 locative phrase order; event-location versus postverbal result-location contrast; static location and source placement
important_limit: The article analyzes locative PP placement, not all predicates, localizers, presentational codas, or Canto Span's umbrella label

citation: Yip, V. and Matthews, S. 2000. Basic Cantonese: A Grammar and Workbook. London/New York: Routledge.
claim_scope: 喺 locative predicates and event locations; localizers; 喺度 location/progressive contrast; 由 source; 向 direction; 經 route/via
important_limit: A teaching reference supplies descriptive examples and contrasts, not corpus frequency, exhaustive diagnostics, or productivity estimates

citation: Leung, H. H. 2014. Cantonese Coverbs: A Syntactic Reanalysis. MA qualifying paper, University of California, Berkeley.
claim_scope: Coverb phrases precede the main verb; inventory includes 喺 location, 向 direction, 由 source, and 經 path/via; category-analysis dispute
important_limit: This unpublished theory paper is corroborative only and cannot by itself promote a parser construction

citation: Matthews, S. 2006. On Serial Verb Constructions in Cantonese. In A. Y. Aikhenvald and R. M. W. Dixon (eds.), Serial Verb Constructions: A Cross-Linguistic Typology, 69-87. Oxford University Press.
claim_scope: Cantonese serial-verb diagnostics; single-clause/single-predicate analysis; subject sharing; purpose-infinitive, compound, complement, and coordination boundaries
important_limit: The chapter explicitly shows that classification may be indeterminate; surface adjacency and subject sharing do not independently prove a purpose relation
